    Alerts & notifications
    Critical — act now
    DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
    Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
    Open the DATP reading room →
    BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
    Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open petition letter →
    Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
    Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open veterans' petition →
    LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
    Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
    Open LDC comment →
    Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
    Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Read background brief →
    HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
    24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
    Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
    Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
    Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
    Open the search portal →
    ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
    HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
    Open district audit dashboard →
    249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
    EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
    See district audit →
    WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
    Platform audit · April 2026
    Draft compliance letter →
    High priority
    All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
    ADA document audit · April 2026
    View ADA analysis →
    0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
    Governance gap · §32-1-202
    See audit dashboard →
    Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
    Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
    See audit dashboard →
    Informational
    2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
    CORA tracker · April 2026
    View tracker →
    Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
    Compliance analysis · April 2026
    Read analysis →
    Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
    New · April 2026
    Find my jurisdiction →
    CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
    New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open blotter dashboard →
    Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
    New · Special Districts · April 2026
    Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
    CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
    New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open CBI form rebuild →
    LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
    New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open ADAM dashboard →

    If this is an emergency, call 911.  ·  AG Office: 720-508-6000  ·  Contact us

    CO
    AG
    Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser · Official complaint portal
    secure .gov

    Complaint Portal

    Portal de Denuncias

    Describe what happened.
    We route it to the right team.

    Write in plain language — tell us what occurred, who was involved, and when. You do not need to know which category or department handles your issue. Every complaint is reviewed by our team and assigned to the right unit.

    Escriba en lenguaje sencillo — cuéntenos qué ocurrió, quién estuvo involucrado y cuándo. No es necesario saber qué categoría o departamento maneja su queja. Cada denuncia es revisada por nuestro equipo y asignada a la unidad correcta.

    →

    How routing works Triage-first intake: Proceso de clasificación: Your narrative is reviewed and classified by our intake team — assisted by Salesforce Einstein AI classification — and assigned to the correct unit: Consumer Fraud, Civil Rights, Environmental Crimes, Medicaid Fraud, or another section. You do not need to self-categorize. Su narrativa es revisada y clasificada por nuestro equipo de recepción, asistido por IA, y asignada a la unidad correcta. No necesita auto-categorizarse.

    Demo mode — configure window.SFCONFIG with your Salesforce org credentials to enable live case creation.

    File a complaint Presentar una denuncia

    Fields marked * are required. All other fields are optional. You may submit anonymously.

    Los campos marcados con * son obligatorios. Puede enviar de forma anónima.

    0 / 4000

    Describe events in the order they happened. Include names, dates, locations, and amounts where relevant. You do not need to know which law was violated — just describe what you witnessed or experienced.

    Describa los eventos en el orden en que ocurrieron. Incluya nombres, fechas, lugares y montos cuando sea relevante.

    Analyzing complaint… suggesting routing unit.
    →
    Online/phone scams? You may also file directly with FTC Report Fraud or StopFraudColorado.gov. You can still submit here — these are additional resources.
    Medicaid fraud? You can also report directly to the HHS Inspector General. Filing here opens a Colorado-level investigation.

    Optional — for case status updates only. Not shared. Opcional — solo para actualizaciones de estado. No se comparte.

    Optional — only used if we need to follow up. Opcional — solo si necesitamos comunicarnos con usted.

    City, county, address, or Colorado region. Helps us route to the correct local office. Ciudad, condado, dirección o región de Colorado. Nos ayuda a dirigir a la oficina correcta.

    Submitted to the Colorado Department of Law. By submitting you certify the information is accurate to the best of your knowledge. Privacy policy ↗ Enviado al Departamento de Ley de Colorado. Al enviar, certifica que la información es precisa según su conocimiento.

    ✓

    Complaint received Denuncia recibida

    Your complaint has been submitted to the Colorado Attorney General's Office and a Salesforce case record has been created. Save your reference number — you may need it for follow-up.

    Su denuncia ha sido enviada a la Fiscalía General de Colorado y se ha creado un registro de caso. Guarde su número de referencia.

    Your reference number Su número de referencia
    Salesforce Case ID
    View case record (opens in new tab) ↗
    • 1 Your complaint is reviewed by the Attorney General's intake team within 3–5 business days. Su denuncia es revisada por el equipo de recepción en 3-5 días hábiles.
    • 2 It is routed to the appropriate section — Consumer Protection, Civil Rights, Environmental, or another unit — based on the complaint content. Se dirige a la sección apropiada según el contenido de la denuncia.
    • 3 If you provided contact information and your complaint warrants follow-up, a representative will contact you. Anonymous complaints are investigated but cannot receive direct responses. Si proporcionó información de contacto, un representante se comunicará con usted si corresponde.
    • 4 The AG cannot represent you individually or provide legal advice. If you need legal representation, visit Colorado Bar Association Lawyer Referral ↗. La Fiscalía no puede representarle individualmente. Si necesita representación legal, visite el Colegio de Abogados de Colorado.

    Other ways to report — by specific issue type Otras formas de reportar — por tipo de problema específico

    No-Call Violations
    Colorado No-Call List
    Registered on the No-Call List but still receiving unwanted telemarketing calls.
    coloradonocall.com →
    Consumer / Phone Scams
    FTC Report Fraud
    Phone scams, online fraud, identity theft. Federal system — data shared with CO AG.
    reportfraud.ftc.gov ↗
    Student Loans
    PSLF Complaint
    Issues with your student loan servicer under the Colorado Student Loan Servicers Act.
    coag.gov/pslf-complaint →
    Insurance Issues
    CO Division of Insurance
    Insurance claims disputes, agent misconduct, or coverage denials.
    doi.colorado.gov ↗
    Speak with Someone
    Contact the AG's Office
    720-508-6000. For situations requiring direct human assistance or interpreter services.
    Contact us →

    This form creates a Salesforce Case record in your Colorado AG Salesforce org using the Salesforce REST API. The narrative is optionally analyzed by the Anthropic Claude API to generate a routing suggestion (mimicking what Salesforce Einstein AI Classification would do in production). In production, OAuth credentials must live on a backend proxy — never in client-side JavaScript.

    Salesforce Object Mapping — Case fields used:

    // POST /services/data/v58.0/sobjects/Case { "Subject": "[AG Complaint] Consumer Fraud — Jefferson County", "Description": "[Citizen narrative text]", "Type": "Consumer Fraud", // from chip selection or AI routing "Status": "New", "Origin": "Web", "Priority": "Normal", "IsAnonymous__c":false, // custom field "Language__c": "English", // preferred language for follow-up "Location__c": "Denver, CO", // incident location custom field "AIRouting__c": "Consumer Protection Unit" // Einstein / Claude suggestion } // Auth flow (backend proxy — never client-side): POST /services/oauth2/token grant_type=client_credentials &client_id=YOUR_CONNECTED_APP_CLIENT_ID &client_secret=YOUR_SECRET // stays on server → returns { access_token, instance_url } // Case response: { "id": "5001000000AbcDEAAZ", "success": true } // Case Number auto-generated: "00001042" etc.

    Recommended backend architecture: Deploy a lightweight Node.js or Python (FastAPI) proxy at /api/complaints/create that accepts the form payload, exchanges client credentials for a Salesforce access token, creates the Case record, and returns the Case Number. The frontend never touches OAuth secrets. For Einstein AI Classification, add a Salesforce Flow that triggers on Case creation to auto-assign Type and OwnerId based on the Description field keywords.

    This demo simulates the API call with a realistic response. Set window.SFCONFIG.demoMode = false and configure proxyEndpoint to enable live Salesforce case creation.

    Get email updates

    We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

    By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
